mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, increasing present values of future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive forward returns do not significantly exceed negative returns when rates decline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer spending power and corporate profit margins, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55 percent of observations where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based aggregate demand growth, which boosts corporate revenues and generates positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive mean return (p > 0.05) over 20-day horizons in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, supply-side constraints force firms to cut capital expenditures and delay hiring, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO declines for two consecutive months, invalidating the supply-constraint profit channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth rates reflect strengthening economic activity that improves near-term corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a rank correlation coefficient consistently near zero across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.5 percent, monetary policy reaches the effective lower bound, eliminating further rate-cut catalysts and triggering negative equity returns as investors price in prolonged stagnation.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5 percent, contradicting the lower-bound stagnation thesis.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The dual labor signal produces negative or flat 60-day forward returns in more than half of triggered instances, breaking the wage-demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below its 2017 baseline index of 100, manufacturing weakness signals deteriorating business investment cycles, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when INDPRO trades below 100, indicating that manufacturing weakness no longer transmits to aggregate equity valuations.
